channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship 2026 at SOAS University of London: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity in Zoroastrian Studies
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The SOAS University of London has officially opened applications for the prestigious Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship 2026, a fully funded opportunity designed for exceptional students interested in pursuing advanced studies connected to Zoroastrianism and related interdisciplinary fields. This scholarship offers full tuition coverage along with a maintenance stipend for one year, making it one of the […]

The post Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship 2026 at SOAS University of London: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity in Zoroastrian Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The SOAS University of London has officially opened applications for the prestigious Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship 2026, a fully funded opportunity designed for exceptional students interested in pursuing advanced studies connected to Zoroastrianism and related interdisciplinary fields.

This scholarship offers full tuition coverage along with a maintenance stipend for one year, making it one of the most valuable master’s funding opportunities currently available for students passionate about Iranian, Middle Eastern, South Asian, religious, political, and cultural studies.

Funded through the generous Shapoorji Pallonji endowment for scholarships in Zoroastrian Studies at SOAS, the award seeks to support academically outstanding students from around the world who are eager to explore Zoroastrianism through diverse academic lenses including history, anthropology, linguistics, religion, and cultural studies.

The application deadline for the scholarship is 29 May 2026.

About the Scholarship

The Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship is hosted by the SOAS Shapoorji Pallonji Institute of Zoroastrian Studies at SOAS University of London.

The scholarship is available for students beginning full-time taught master’s programmes during the 2026/27 academic year.

Selected scholars will receive:

  • Full tuition fee coverage
  • A maintenance stipend for living expenses
  • Access to world-class academic resources at SOAS
  • The opportunity to specialize in Zoroastrian Studies within a globally respected institution
  • Exposure to interdisciplinary approaches to religion, politics, history, language, and society

The scholarship aims to encourage innovative academic engagement with Zoroastrianism and related fields while supporting future researchers, scholars, and professionals in the humanities and social sciences.

Eligible Master’s Programmes

Applicants can choose from the following eligible one-year full-time master’s programmes:

  1. MA Iranian Studies
  2. MA Religion, Politics and Society
  3. MA South Asian Area Studies
  4. MA Middle Eastern Studies

Students awarded the scholarship will also be required to complete:

  • A compulsory 60-credit dissertation in Zoroastrianism
  • The 30-credit module:
    “Zoroastrianism, Historical and Contemporary Perspectives”

Additionally, students may choose to study an extra language module such as Avestan language studies.

Scholarship Benefits

The Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship offers substantial academic and financial support, including:

1. Full Tuition Coverage

The scholarship fully covers tuition fees for the selected master’s programme.

2. Maintenance Stipend

Recipients also receive financial support toward living costs during their studies in London.

3. Academic Development

Students gain access to internationally respected faculty, specialized research opportunities, and interdisciplinary learning environments.

4. Research Opportunities

The programme supports students interested in:

  • Historical studies
  • Religious studies
  • Anthropology
  • Linguistics
  • Cultural studies
  • Middle Eastern and South Asian studies

5. Global Recognition

Studying at SOAS offers international academic exposure and networking opportunities within global research communities.

Who Can Apply?

The scholarship is open to both:

  • Home students
  • Overseas/international students

Applicants from any country may apply provided they meet the academic and admission requirements.

Academic Requirements

To qualify, applicants should:

  • Hold or expect to receive a first-class honours degree from a UK university or an international equivalent
  • Demonstrate strong academic excellence through transcripts and references
  • Have applied for an eligible master’s programme at SOAS
  • Meet English language requirements if applicable

Applicants with a 2:1 degree classification may still apply, although preference is generally given to candidates with first-class academic performance.

Important Admission Conditions

Applicants should note the following carefully:

  • You do not need to receive an admission offer before submitting your scholarship application.
  • However, you must obtain an unconditional admission offer before the scholarship selection panel meets.
  • English language conditions must also be fulfilled before final consideration.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply for admission as early as possible because programme assessments may take:

  • Up to 4 weeks under normal circumstances
  • Up to 6 weeks during busy periods

Late or incomplete scholarship applications will not be considered.

Scholarship Deadline

Scholarship Application Deadline:

29 May 2026 (12 noon UK local time)

Applicants are advised to complete both:

  1. Their programme application
  2. Their scholarship application

well before the deadline to avoid delays.

How to Apply

Step 1: Apply for an Eligible Master’s Programme

Candidates must first submit an application for one of the eligible master’s programmes at SOAS.

Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship

After applying for admission, applicants can access the scholarship application through the SOAS admissions portal using their login credentials.

Applicants who cannot see the scholarship option in the portal should verify that they meet all eligibility requirements.

Candidate Assessment Process

Applications will be reviewed based on:

  • Academic excellence
  • Personal statement quality
  • Proposed area of study
  • References and transcripts
  • Overall suitability for Zoroastrian Studies research

The scholarship selection panel will carefully assess each candidate’s academic potential and alignment with the objectives of the programme.

Notification of Results

The scholarship selection process is expected to conclude around the end of May 2026 or shortly afterward.

  • Successful candidates will be contacted first.
  • Unsuccessful applicants will be notified once all awards have been allocated.

Why This Scholarship Matters

The Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship represents a major opportunity for students interested in exploring religion, culture, history, politics, and society through an interdisciplinary and globally relevant perspective.

With rising international tuition costs, fully funded opportunities like this are highly competitive and valuable. The programme also allows students to engage deeply with Zoroastrian traditions and their influence across regions such as Iran, South Asia, and the Middle East.

For students seeking careers in academia, policy, cultural studies, international relations, religious studies, or heritage research, this scholarship can serve as a transformative academic pathway.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post Shapoorji Pallonji Scholarship 2026 at SOAS University of London: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity in Zoroastrian Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Bank East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026: FutureMakers-Youth Driving Jobs Through Digital Innovation and Entrepreneurship
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The World Bank Group is set to host the highly anticipated East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026, themed #FutureMakers: Youth Driving Jobs, bringing together young leaders, policymakers, business executives, development partners, and global experts to discuss the future of employment in the digital era. This major international forum is designed to empower young people […]

The post World Bank East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026: FutureMakers-Youth Driving Jobs Through Digital Innovation and Entrepreneurship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Bank Group is set to host the highly anticipated East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026, themed #FutureMakers: Youth Driving Jobs, bringing together young leaders, policymakers, business executives, development partners, and global experts to discuss the future of employment in the digital era.

This major international forum is designed to empower young people across the East Asia and Pacific region by helping them understand emerging job trends, gain practical insights into digital transformation, explore entrepreneurship opportunities, and build meaningful professional networks.

As economies continue evolving rapidly due to artificial intelligence, automation, and technological innovation, the forum serves as a strategic platform for preparing the next generation of youth to thrive in the changing labor market.

About the East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026

The East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum is a large-scale regional event focused on addressing one of the most urgent issues facing young people today: the future of jobs.

The forum seeks to equip youth with:

  • Knowledge about labor market trends
  • Skills needed for future careers
  • Opportunities for collaboration and innovation
  • Access to global conversations on employment and digital transformation

The 2026 edition places strong emphasis on:

  • Digital innovation
  • Entrepreneurship
  • AI-powered jobs
  • Skills development
  • Employment pathways
  • Youth-led solutions

Event Date

20 – 21 May 2026 (ET)

Event Format

The forum will be conducted in a hybrid format, allowing participants to join:

  • Virtually from anywhere in the world
  • In-person through designated event locations

Registered participants will receive livestream details closer to the event date.

The Growing Jobs Challenge in East Asia and Pacific

One of the key highlights of the forum is its focus on the region’s widening employment gap.

According to the event information:

  • More than 320 million young people across East Asia and Pacific are expected to enter the workforce over the next decade
  • Only about 110 million jobs are projected to be available

This creates a gap of approximately 210 million job opportunities, posing significant economic and social challenges across the region.

The forum aims to tackle these concerns by encouraging dialogue, innovation, collaboration, and practical solutions that can support sustainable youth employment.

What Participants Can Expect

The Youth Forum 2026 offers an engaging and highly interactive experience designed for emerging leaders and changemakers.

1. Understanding the Future Job Landscape

Participants will gain valuable insights into:

  • The future of work in East Asia and Pacific
  • How digital transformation is reshaping industries
  • Opportunities and risks in AI-driven economies
  • Emerging career pathways for youth

2. Interactive Learning Sessions

The forum will feature:

  • Skills development workshops
  • Entrepreneurship discussions
  • Career readiness sessions
  • Digital transformation training
  • Collaborative problem-solving activities

These sessions will be led by:

  • Partner organizations
  • Industry leaders
  • Government representatives
  • Development experts
  • World Bank Group specialists

3. Networking Opportunities

Attendees will have opportunities to:

  • Connect with peers from across the region
  • Meet professionals and business leaders
  • Engage with policymakers and development experts
  • Share ideas and innovative solutions

Networking sessions are expected to help young participants build relationships that could support future collaborations, careers, and entrepreneurial ventures.

4. Exploring Digital and AI-Powered Careers

A major focus of the event is the role of technology in creating employment opportunities.

Participants will explore:

  • AI-driven job opportunities
  • Digital entrepreneurship
  • Technology-enabled employment pathways
  • Skills needed for the digital economy
  • Apprenticeships and innovation ecosystems

Why This Forum Matters

The East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum is more than just a conference—it is a platform for youth empowerment and policy dialogue.

The event is important because it:

  • Addresses the growing youth unemployment crisis
  • Encourages youth participation in economic transformation
  • Promotes scalable employment solutions
  • Supports entrepreneurship and innovation
  • Bridges skills gaps through digital learning

It also creates opportunities for youth voices to influence regional conversations on jobs, education, and economic development.

Connection to the World Bank Global Youth Summit

The event also serves as a lead-up initiative to the World Bank Group Global Youth Summit, scheduled for: 11 – 12 June 2026

This connection expands opportunities for participants to remain engaged in global youth development conversations beyond the regional forum.

Who Should Attend?

The forum is ideal for:

  • Students
  • Young professionals
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Youth leaders
  • Innovators
  • Career seekers
  • Individuals interested in digital transformation and future jobs

Participants interested in entrepreneurship, technology, workforce development, and social impact will particularly benefit from the event.

Virtual Participation and Registration

The event offers virtual participation options, making it accessible to participants from different countries and backgrounds.

Interested individuals are encouraged to register early to secure participation access.

Registration Link: Click here

Benefits of Participating

Participants can benefit from:

  • Exposure to global employment trends
  • Learning from industry and policy experts
  • Enhanced understanding of future career opportunities
  • Networking with regional and international stakeholders
  • Insights into entrepreneurship and innovation
  • Access to discussions on AI and digital jobs

Final Thoughts

The World Bank East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026 represents a timely and impactful opportunity for young people to prepare for the future workforce.

As technology continues transforming economies globally, youth must adapt, innovate, and lead. Through interactive learning, networking, entrepreneurship discussions, and policy engagement, the forum aims to empower participants with the tools and knowledge needed to succeed in an increasingly digital world.

Young changemakers across the region and beyond should strongly consider participating in this transformative event.

For more information, click HERE

Discover more global conference opportunities on OFY, VISIT

The post World Bank East Asia and Pacific Youth Forum 2026: FutureMakers-Youth Driving Jobs Through Digital Innovation and Entrepreneurship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: New Profit Connected Futures 2026 Cohort: $100,000 Unrestricted Grant and Leadership Support for U.S.-Based Social Impact Organizations
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Organizations working to bridge divides, strengthen collaboration, and create meaningful social change across communities in the United States now have a major funding and capacity-building opportunity through the newly launched Connected Futures cohort by New Profit. The Connected Futures initiative is part of New Profit’s Catalyze program, which supports innovative social impact organizations dedicated to […]

The post New Profit Connected Futures 2026 Cohort: $100,000 Unrestricted Grant and Leadership Support for U.S.-Based Social Impact Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to bridge divides, strengthen collaboration, and create meaningful social change across communities in the United States now have a major funding and capacity-building opportunity through the newly launched Connected Futures cohort by New Profit.

The Connected Futures initiative is part of New Profit’s Catalyze program, which supports innovative social impact organizations dedicated to expanding access and opportunity across America. Through this funding cycle, selected organizations will receive unrestricted grants, leadership development funding, strategic advisory support, and access to a national peer-learning network.

This initiative is specifically designed for organizations helping people overcome mistrust, polarization, exclusion, and social disconnection in order to solve problems collectively and build stronger communities.

About the Connected Futures Cohort

Connected Futures is a newly launched Catalyze cohort developed by New Profit to support organizations that help people move across active divides toward collective action and collaboration.

The initiative recognizes that many communities face increasing polarization, mistrust, and fragmentation, making it difficult for individuals and groups to work together toward shared goals. Connected Futures seeks to invest in organizations building the relationships, structures, skills, and trust needed to strengthen civic participation and social cohesion.

Unlike traditional funding opportunities focused on a single issue area, Connected Futures supports organizations united by a common bridge-building approach rather than a single thematic sector.

Organizations may operate within areas such as:

  • Democracy
  • Economic mobility
  • Education
  • Physical health
  • Mental health
  • Community development
  • Civic engagement
  • Social cohesion

What matters most is that the organization actively helps people collaborate across differences and create pathways for collective problem-solving.

What Selected Organizations Will Receive

Organizations selected for the Connected Futures cohort will benefit from a comprehensive support package that combines funding, leadership development, strategic advising, and peer learning.

1. One-Year $100,000 Unrestricted Grant

Each selected organization will receive:

  • A one-year unrestricted grant of $100,000
  • Flexible funding that can be used to strengthen operations, programs, staffing, or organizational growth

Unrestricted grants provide organizations with the flexibility needed to address operational realities and scale impact effectively.

2. Additional $10,000 Leadership Development Grant

Social Entrepreneurs leading selected organizations will also receive:

  • A $10,000 grant dedicated to leadership development
  • Resources to support executive coaching, leadership training, wellness, professional growth, or strategic learning opportunities

3. Strategic Advisory Support

Participating organizations will receive tailored guidance from the New Profit team through:

  • One-on-one strategic advising
  • Organizational development support
  • Fundraising guidance
  • Strategic planning assistance
  • Board development workshops
  • Storytelling and communications support
  • Scenario planning support

4. Peer Learning and Community Building

Selected Social Entrepreneurs will participate in a collaborative cohort experience that includes:

  • Peer-to-peer learning opportunities
  • Leadership networking
  • Shared problem-solving
  • Capacity-building workshops
  • National convenings

What Is the Catalyze Support Model?

The Catalyze support model is New Profit’s year-long investment and learning framework for innovative social entrepreneurs.

Through Catalyze cohorts, New Profit has already invested in:

  • More than 250 organizations
  • Organizations serving over 35 million people
  • Community-centered initiatives across all 50 U.S. states

According to New Profit, 86% of participating Social Entrepreneurs reported that the Catalyze model helped amplify their impact.

Participation Requirements for Selected Organizations

Organizations selected for the cohort are expected to fully engage in the year-long program experience.

In-Person Convenings

Social Entrepreneurs must attend:

  • Three in-person convenings
  • Each convening lasts 3–4 days
  • Events will take place in various cities across the United States

New Profit covers:

  • Travel expenses
  • Accommodation costs
  • Meals during convenings

Virtual Learning Sessions

Participants must also attend:

  • Three virtual learning sessions
  • Each session lasts approximately 2–3 hours

Strategic Advising Sessions

Social Entrepreneurs will engage in:

  • At least six virtual one-on-one advising sessions with the New Profit team

Estimated Time Commitment

The program requires approximately:

  • 6–8 hours per month on average

Who Is a Social Entrepreneur According to New Profit?

New Profit defines a Social Entrepreneur as the leader and primary decision-maker of an organization.

This individual may hold titles such as:

  • Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
  • Executive Director
  • Founder
  • Managing Director
  • Equivalent executive leadership roles

Organizations with co-leadership structures are also eligible for consideration.

Eligibility Criteria for the Connected Futures Cohort

Organizations interested in the Connected Futures 2026 grantmaking cycle must meet several eligibility requirements.

1. Focus on the United States

Eligible organizations must:

  • Operate in the United States or U.S. territories
  • Primarily serve communities within the United States

2. 501(c)(3) Status

Organizations must:

  • Be an independent 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization
  • Or have a fiscal sponsor with 501(c)(3) status

3. Annual Expenses Requirement

Organizations must report annual expenses between:

  • $250,000 and $2 million

This should reflect the organization’s most recently closed fiscal year.

4. Full-Time Leadership Requirement

Organizations must be led by at least one full-time Social Entrepreneur working approximately:

  • 30 hours per week or more

5. Minimum Operational History

Organizations must have:

  • Operated their program or impact model for at least two years

However, New Profit clarified that organizations do not need to have held 501(c)(3) status for the full two-year period as long as they currently meet the legal requirement at the time of submission.

6. No Previous Funding from New Profit

Organizations are not eligible if they have previously received investment from:

  • New Profit Build initiatives
  • New Profit Catalyze initiatives

7. Strategic Alignment Requirement

Organizations must align with New Profit’s strategic priorities by demonstrating how they:

  • Build trust across divides
  • Encourage productive dialogue
  • Support civic participation
  • Strengthen relationships and belonging
  • Facilitate collaborative decision-making
  • Promote collective action and co-design

Types of Divides Organizations May Address

Connected Futures seeks organizations working across various forms of social division, including:

  • Political divides
  • Racial divides
  • Ethnic divides
  • Religious divides
  • Economic divides
  • Geographic divides
  • Generational divides
  • Ideological divides

The emphasis is on helping communities move from mistrust and disconnection toward collaboration and shared problem-solving.

Important Deadline

Organizations interested in the Connected Futures 2026 cohort must submit the Discovery Form by:

Tuesday, May 26, 2026, at 2:00 PM PT / 5:00 PM ET

Late submissions may not be considered for the 2026 funding cycle.

How to Apply

Organizations must first complete a Discovery Form to indicate interest in the Connected Futures grantmaking cycle.

Important notes:

  • Submitting a Discovery Form is not the same as submitting a full application
  • Organizations selected as strong strategic fits will later be invited to complete a full application
  • Invitations for full applications are expected to be sent in late June 2026

When completing the Discovery Form:

  1. Select “Cycle-specific submission”
  2. Search for “Connected Futures 2026”

Official Links

New Profit Connected Futures Information Page

For questions and support:

About New Profit

New Profit is a venture philanthropy organization focused on catalyzing the impact of social entrepreneurs expanding access and opportunity in America.

Since 1998, the organization has:

  • Invested more than $350 million
  • Supported over 275 high-impact organizations
  • Reached more than 35 million people across all 50 states

New Profit provides organizations with:

  • Capital
  • Capacity-building support
  • Strategic advising
  • Leadership development
  • Community and networking opportunities

Why Organizations Should Apply

The Connected Futures cohort offers more than funding. It provides organizations with:

  • Flexible unrestricted financial support
  • National visibility
  • Executive leadership development
  • Strategic mentorship
  • Networking opportunities
  • Long-term organizational strengthening
  • Access to a national community of changemakers

For organizations dedicated to rebuilding trust, strengthening civic collaboration, and addressing systemic challenges collectively, this opportunity can significantly expand impact and sustainability.

 

Apply here

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Grant opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post New Profit Connected Futures 2026 Cohort: $100,000 Unrestricted Grant and Leadership Support for U.S.-Based Social Impact Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Postdoctoral Research Associate at Northeastern University, USA: Fully Funded Health Communication and Health Equity Research Opportunity for Global Scholars
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Researchers and early-career academics seeking to advance their careers in health communication, public health, and health equity now have an exciting opportunity to join one of the leading research institutions in the United States. Northeastern University is recruiting a Postdoctoral Research Associate to support cutting-edge transdisciplinary research within the Communication for Health Equity Lab led […]

The post Postdoctoral Research Associate at Northeastern University, USA: Fully Funded Health Communication and Health Equity Research Opportunity for Global Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers and early-career academics seeking to advance their careers in health communication, public health, and health equity now have an exciting opportunity to join one of the leading research institutions in the United States. Northeastern University is recruiting a Postdoctoral Research Associate to support cutting-edge transdisciplinary research within the Communication for Health Equity Lab led by Professor Diane Francis.

This prestigious research position is designed for scholars passionate about health behavior change, culturally responsive communication interventions, and reducing health disparities among underserved populations. The role offers an opportunity to contribute to impactful research projects while building expertise in mixed methods research, AI-driven health communication, digital media, community engagement, and health campaign evaluation.

The appointment is for two years and is based in Boston, Massachusetts, USA.

About the Postdoctoral Research Associate Position

The Communication for Health Equity Lab focuses on advancing health and well-being through effective communication strategies. The selected Postdoctoral Research Associate will collaborate on innovative projects that explore how communication interventions can improve health outcomes for populations affected by health disparities.

The lab’s research combines communication science, public health, technology, and behavioral sciences to create evidence-based solutions that promote equity in healthcare access and health information dissemination.

This position is especially valuable for researchers interested in:

  • Health communication
  • Public health interventions
  • Computational health communication
  • Artificial intelligence in health messaging
  • Social and digital media impacts on health outcomes
  • Community-centered health research
  • Mixed methods research
  • Health equity and underserved populations

Key Responsibilities

The successful candidate will play an active role in various research and academic activities within the Communication for Health Equity Lab.

Responsibilities include:

1. Conducting Research on Health Communication Strategies

The researcher will contribute to projects focused on:

  • Developing health communication interventions
  • Evaluating communication strategies
  • Addressing health disparities
  • Supporting underserved populations
  • Designing culturally responsive health messaging

2. Utilizing Mixed Methods Research

The position requires expertise or interest in multiple research methodologies, including:

  • Qualitative research methods
  • Quantitative research approaches
  • Community engagement research
  • Big data collection and analytics
  • Population-based surveys
  • Health campaign evaluation studies

3. Grant Writing and Research Development

The postdoctoral associate will assist with:

  • Developing research proposals
  • Writing grant applications
  • Designing new research initiatives
  • Supporting collaborative academic projects

4. Academic Writing and Publications

The role also involves:

  • Manuscript preparation
  • Scientific publication writing
  • Disseminating research findings
  • Supporting scholarly communication

5. Lab Management and Student Supervision

The selected candidate will help oversee research activities and mentor students by:

  • Supervising graduate students
  • Supporting undergraduate researchers
  • Assisting with lab coordination
  • Managing research workflows

Minimum Qualifications

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • A PhD degree (or expected completion before the start date)
  • Academic background in:
    • Communication
    • Public health
    • Health promotion
    • Social and behavioral sciences
    • Or related fields

Candidates with interests in the following areas will have an added advantage:

  • Computational health communication
  • Artificial intelligence and health messaging
  • Social media and health outcomes
  • Digital communication research

Preferred Qualifications

Northeastern University is particularly interested in applicants who possess:

  • Experience conducting mixed methods research
  • Expertise in qualitative and quantitative analysis
  • Experience with population-based surveys
  • Knowledge of big data analytics
  • Experience evaluating health communication campaigns

The institution strongly encourages applications from individuals from diverse backgrounds, particularly groups underrepresented in health-related research.

Salary and Benefits

The Postdoctoral Research Associate position comes with a competitive salary package and employee benefits.

Expected Salary Range

  • USD 59,423.00 – USD 83,934.50 annually

The final salary will depend on:

  • Educational qualifications
  • Professional experience
  • Skills and expertise
  • Geographic considerations
  • Internal salary comparisons

Employee Benefits

Eligible employees at Northeastern University receive comprehensive benefits, including:

  • Medical insurance
  • Dental coverage
  • Vision insurance
  • Paid time off
  • Retirement plans
  • Wellness and life programs
  • Tuition assistance
  • Transportation and commuting support

More information about employee benefits can be found on the official Northeastern University benefits page:

Northeastern University Benefits Information

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

This postdoctoral opportunity offers several major advantages for researchers aiming to build international academic careers.

Career Advancement

Researchers will gain:

  • High-level academic research experience
  • Publication opportunities
  • Grant writing exposure
  • International collaboration experience
  • Mentorship and leadership opportunities

Exposure to Innovative Research

The role integrates emerging areas such as:

  • AI-driven health communication
  • Big data in public health
  • Digital health interventions
  • Social media analytics
  • Community-based participatory research

Work Within a Prestigious Institution

Northeastern University is internationally recognized for excellence in research, innovation, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

Application Process

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official Northeastern University careers portal.

Official Application Link

Apply for the Postdoctoral Research Associate Position

Applicants are encouraged to prepare:

  • Updated academic CV
  • Cover letter
  • Research statement
  • Publication samples
  • References

Important Information

  • Position Type: Research
  • Duration: 2 years
  • Location: Boston, Massachusetts, USA
  • Employer: Northeastern University
  • Research Area: Health Communication and Health Equity
  • Salary Range: USD 59,423 – USD 83,934.50

Deadline

No specific application deadline was mentioned in the announcement. However, interested applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible because the job posting has already been published.

Conclusion

The Postdoctoral Research Associate opportunity at Northeastern University presents an outstanding pathway for researchers passionate about health communication, public health, and health equity. With access to interdisciplinary research, advanced methodologies, mentorship opportunities, and competitive benefits, this position offers an ideal environment for early-career scholars to grow academically and professionally.

Researchers with expertise in communication sciences, AI-driven health messaging, public health, and mixed methods research should strongly consider applying for this prestigious opportunity in the United States.

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Postdoctoral Research Associate at Northeastern University, USA: Fully Funded Health Communication and Health Equity Research Opportunity for Global Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WHO/Europe 2026 Mental Health Capacity-Building Programme for Public Health Professionals in Europe | Fully Accredited Training Opportunity in Bilbao, Spain
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Mental health promotion and prevention are becoming increasingly important priorities across Europe, especially as governments, public health agencies, and institutions work to address rising mental health challenges affecting communities. In response to this growing need, World Health Organization Regional Office for Europe (WHO/Europe), in collaboration with the European Public Health Association (EUPHA), has launched a […]

The post WHO/Europe 2026 Mental Health Capacity-Building Programme for Public Health Professionals in Europe | Fully Accredited Training Opportunity in Bilbao, Spain appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Mental health promotion and prevention are becoming increasingly important priorities across Europe, especially as governments, public health agencies, and institutions work to address rising mental health challenges affecting communities. In response to this growing need, World Health Organization Regional Office for Europe (WHO/Europe), in collaboration with the European Public Health Association (EUPHA), has launched a major call for applications for its 2026 Capacity-Building Programme on Strengthening Public Health Workforce Capacity for Mental Health Promotion and Prevention.

This prestigious programme is designed to equip public health professionals with practical, evidence-based tools and strategies for advancing mental health promotion, prevention, anti-stigma action, and resilience-building across health systems and communities.

The initiative forms part of a broader project supported by the European Commission titled “Addressing mental health challenges in the European Union, Iceland and Norway.”

The programme combines:

  • A 6-week interactive webinar series
  • An in-person synthesis conference in Bilbao, Spain
  • Accredited public health education credits
  • Opportunities for networking and collaboration across Europe

The first cohort will include 120 professionals from European Union countries, Iceland, and Norway.

Deadline for Applications

Applications must be submitted no later than 26 May 2026.

Selected participants will be notified on 5 July 2026.

 

About the WHO/Europe Mental Health Programme

WHO/Europe recognizes that mental health promotion and prevention are no longer optional additions to public health systems — they are essential public health responsibilities.

Recent consultations conducted by WHO/Europe and its partners identified several major challenges affecting mental health systems across Europe, including:

  • Limited understanding of effective population-level mental health interventions
  • Weak integration between clinical mental health services and public health sectors
  • Persistent stigma surrounding mental health, even within professional institutions
  • Insufficient use of lived experience perspectives in programme design
  • Gaps in mental health surveillance and monitoring systems
  • Limited measurement tools beyond service utilization and prevalence statistics

To address these gaps, WHO/Europe and EUPHA developed this specialized training programme aimed at strengthening the skills and competencies of public health professionals working on mental health policy, practice, research, and strategy.

 

What the Programme Offers

The WHO/Europe capacity-building programme is structured around two major components:

  1. Interactive Webinar Series

Participants will attend five interactive webinars between 10 September and 15 October 2026.

Sessions will take place from 11:30–13:00 Central European Summer Time (CEST).

The webinar topics include:

  1. Strategies for addressing and reducing stigma
  2. Advancing research on mental health prevention
  3. Intersectoral collaboration and “Mental Health in All Policies”
  4. Integrating lived and living experience into public mental health action
  5. Mental health surveillance, data integration, and monitoring systems

These webinars are designed to provide participants with practical, research-informed tools that can be implemented within their institutions and communities.

 

  1. In-Person Synthesis Conference in Bilbao, Spain

Following the webinar series, participants will be invited to attend a one-day synthesis conference titled:

“Strengthening Public Health Practice in Mental Health”

The event will take place on:

  • 10 November 2026
  • Bilbao, Spain

The conference will serve as a pre-conference event for the European Public Health Conference.

The in-person event will allow participants to:

  • Consolidate learning from the webinar sessions
  • Engage in collaborative discussions
  • Exchange practical experiences and solutions
  • Build professional networks
  • Explore future actions for public mental health systems

Participants may also separately register for the broader European Public Health Conference, subject to conference registration requirements.

 

Accreditation and Certification Benefits

One of the strongest aspects of this programme is its internationally recognized accreditation system.

Successful participants will receive:

  • An EUPHAcademy Certificate
  • 7.5 APHEA credits for completing the webinar series
  • Up to 12.5 APHEA credits for participating in both the webinar series and the synthesis conference

The curriculum aligns with:

  • The WHO–ASPHER Competency Framework for the Public Health Workforce in the European Region (2020)
  • The ASPHER Core Curriculum for Public Health (2024)

This makes the programme highly valuable for professionals seeking career advancement in public health and mental health sectors.

 

Learning Objectives

The programme aims to help participants:

  • Understand mental health from a population health perspective
  • Apply evidence-informed approaches to mental health promotion and prevention
  • Improve collaboration between public health and psychiatric professionals
  • Integrate lived experience perspectives into mental health action
  • Strengthen public mental health systems
  • Promote “mental health in all policies” approaches
  • Use appropriate indicators and surveillance tools for monitoring mental health outcomes

 

Who Should Apply?

The programme is specifically designed for public health professionals involved in:

  • Policy development
  • Public health practice
  • Research
  • Strategic health planning
  • Community health systems
  • Mental health promotion initiatives

Typical eligible roles include:

  • Policy officers
  • Public health officers
  • Epidemiologists
  • Surveillance and data analysts
  • Community health workers
  • Health promotion practitioners
  • Researchers
  • Strategic communicators
  • Cross-system liaison officers

Applicants may come from:

  • National public health institutes
  • Ministries of health
  • Ministries of social affairs
  • Regional and local health authorities
  • Universities and research institutions
  • Community-based health organizations

 

Important Eligibility Information

Accreditation eligibility is restricted to professionals currently working in:

  • European Union Member States
  • Iceland
  • Norway

However, WHO/Europe has also announced that:

  • Recorded sessions
  • Learning resources
  • Programme materials

will be openly accessible to individuals worldwide without an application requirement.

This means global learners and mental health advocates can still benefit from programme content even if they are not eligible for formal accreditation.

 

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following documents through the EUPHAcademy online registration system:

  1. Cover Letter (Maximum 500 Words)

The cover letter must explain:

  • Current role and organization
  • Connection between the applicant’s work and mental health promotion/prevention
  • Learning expectations from the programme
  • How the knowledge gained will be applied
  • Plans for sharing the learning with colleagues or institutions
  1. Curriculum Vitae (Maximum 2 Pages)

The CV must follow GDPR-compliant standards and should NOT include:

  • Home address
  • Phone number
  • Date of birth
  • Photograph
  • Marital status
  • Other personal identifiers

 

Selection Criteria

WHO/Europe and EUPHA aim to ensure:

  • Balanced geographical representation
  • Professional relevance
  • Diversity of expertise
  • Meaningful inclusion of people with lived and living experience

Because places are limited, applicants are encouraged to prepare strong and compelling applications.

 

Why This Programme Matters

Mental health has become one of the most urgent public health priorities globally. Public health systems increasingly require professionals who understand:

  • Prevention strategies
  • Community-based interventions
  • Anti-stigma frameworks
  • Cross-sector collaboration
  • Data-driven mental health policies

This programme offers participants a unique opportunity to gain specialized competencies while connecting with leading public health experts and institutions across Europe.

For professionals working in health systems, policy, research, and mental health advocacy, this initiative could significantly strengthen both technical expertise and professional networks.

 

Application Link

Interested applicants can apply through the official EUPHAcademy registration platform provided by WHO/Europe and EUPHA.

For more information, visit:

WHO/Europe Official Page

 

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Fellowship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post WHO/Europe 2026 Mental Health Capacity-Building Programme for Public Health Professionals in Europe | Fully Accredited Training Opportunity in Bilbao, Spain appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNDP GEF Small Grants Programme 2026: Funding Opportunity for Environmental and Climate Projects in Mozambique
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
The United Nations Development Programme has officially launched the GEF Small Grants Programme (GEF-SGP) 2026 in Mozambique, inviting eligible organisations to submit innovative project proposals focused on environmental sustainability, climate resilience, biodiversity conservation, and community-driven development. This funding opportunity represents a major platform for grassroots organisations, civil society groups, and community-based initiatives seeking to implement […]

The post UNDP GEF Small Grants Programme 2026: Funding Opportunity for Environmental and Climate Projects in Mozambique appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Development Programme has officially launched the GEF Small Grants Programme (GEF-SGP) 2026 in Mozambique, inviting eligible organisations to submit innovative project proposals focused on environmental sustainability, climate resilience, biodiversity conservation, and community-driven development.

This funding opportunity represents a major platform for grassroots organisations, civil society groups, and community-based initiatives seeking to implement impactful environmental and climate projects that improve both ecosystems and local livelihoods.

Implemented globally in partnership with the Global Environment Facility (GEF), the programme has become one of the most influential community-level environmental funding initiatives worldwide.

About the UNDP GEF Small Grants Programme (GEF-SGP)

The Global Environment Facility Small Grants Programme is a globally recognized initiative that supports community-led environmental action and sustainable development.

With more than three decades of implementation across nearly 100 countries, the programme has funded thousands of local environmental projects aimed at tackling major global challenges such as:

  • Climate change
  • Biodiversity loss
  • Land degradation
  • Ecosystem destruction
  • Unsustainable natural resource management

In Mozambique, the programme has been implemented by UNDP since 2005 and has already supported more than 287 projects focused on environmental protection and socio-economic development.

The 2026 funding cycle continues this mission by empowering local organisations to design and implement innovative, sustainable, and community-centered solutions.

Purpose of the 2026 Call for Proposals

The 2026 call for proposals aims to:

  1. Support grassroots environmental innovation
  2. Strengthen climate resilience in vulnerable communities
  3. Protect ecologically important landscapes
  4. Improve sustainable natural resource management
  5. Promote community participation in environmental governance
  6. Generate sustainable socio-economic benefits

The programme places strong emphasis on projects that combine environmental sustainability with local development impact.

Priority Focus Areas

Applicants are encouraged to develop projects that address key environmental and climate-related challenges.

Eligible Project Themes Include

1. Biodiversity Conservation

Projects may focus on:

  • Protecting endangered ecosystems
  • Conserving wildlife habitats
  • Supporting ecological restoration

2. Sustainable Land and Water Management

Proposals can include:

  • Soil conservation
  • Sustainable agriculture practices
  • Water resource protection

3. Climate Change Adaptation and Mitigation

Projects may address:

  • Community climate resilience
  • Carbon reduction initiatives
  • Adaptation strategies for vulnerable populations

4. Ecosystem Restoration

The programme supports:

  • Restoration of terrestrial ecosystems
  • Wetland and aquatic ecosystem recovery
  • Reforestation and rehabilitation activities

5. Community-Based Natural Resource Management

Applicants can propose:

  • Community-led conservation models
  • Sustainable livelihood initiatives
  • Participatory environmental governance systems

Geographic Areas of Focus

For the 2026 funding cycle, UNDP Mozambique prioritizes projects located in the following ecological landscapes:

1. Chimanimani-Gorongosa-Marromeu Landscape

Located in Sofala Province, this area is known for its ecological importance and biodiversity value.

2. Matutuine Region

Located in Maputo Province, this region faces important environmental and climate-related challenges requiring sustainable interventions.

Projects implemented within these areas are strongly encouraged.

Funding and Capacity Building Benefits

The GEF Small Grants Programme is not limited to financial support alone. It also emphasizes institutional strengthening and technical capacity development.

Programme Benefits

Selected organisations may benefit from:

  • Grant funding for environmental projects
  • Technical guidance and support
  • Capacity-building opportunities
  • Access to environmental knowledge resources
  • Strengthened project implementation skills
  • Enhanced organisational development

The programme follows a practical “learning-by-doing” model that helps organisations improve both technical expertise and long-term sustainability.

Who Can Apply?

The call is open to organisations operating within Mozambique, including:

  • Civil Society Organisations (CSOs)
  • Community-Based Organisations (CBOs)
  • Non-Governmental Organisations (NGOs)

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • Capacity to implement community-focused projects
  • Alignment with environmental and development objectives
  • Strong community engagement approaches

The programme particularly values projects that are locally driven and community-oriented.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Environmental degradation and climate change continue to threaten ecosystems, livelihoods, and economic development across Africa.

The GEF-SGP helps bridge the gap between global environmental goals and local action by enabling communities themselves to lead sustainable solutions.

This initiative is important because it:

  • Empowers grassroots organisations
  • Encourages local innovation
  • Supports sustainable development goals
  • Strengthens climate resilience
  • Enhances biodiversity protection
  • Promotes inclusive environmental governance

Long-Term Impact of the Programme

The programme has already demonstrated strong impact globally and within Mozambique.

Key Outcomes of Previous Projects

Past initiatives have contributed to:

  • Improved livelihoods
  • Better natural resource management
  • Increased climate resilience
  • Community empowerment
  • Ecosystem conservation
  • Sustainable economic opportunities

By investing in local solutions, the programme ensures long-lasting environmental and socio-economic benefits.

Application Process

Interested organisations must submit their proposals via email.

Submission Email

Submissions through: gefsgp.mz@undp.org 

Application Deadline

Applications are open from: 1 May 2026 – 30 May 2026

Applicants are advised to carefully review all relevant documents, including:

  • Terms of Reference
  • Proposal templates
  • Programme strategy guidelines

before submitting their applications.

How to Prepare a Strong Application

To improve chances of selection, applicants should:

  1. Clearly define the environmental problem
  2. Demonstrate community involvement
  3. Present measurable environmental outcomes
  4. Show long-term sustainability plans
  5. Align projects with local ecosystem priorities
  6. Include realistic implementation strategies

Projects that combine environmental protection with socio-economic benefits are likely to stand out.

Final Thoughts

The UNDP GEF Small Grants Programme 2026 offers a powerful opportunity for Mozambican organisations to access funding and technical support for transformative environmental and climate initiatives.

As climate challenges continue to intensify globally, community-led action remains essential for achieving sustainable development and protecting vulnerable ecosystems.

Organisations with innovative, locally driven ideas should strongly consider applying before the deadline and contribute to building a more resilient and sustainable future for Mozambique.

For more information about the opportunity, click HERE

Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT

The post UNDP GEF Small Grants Programme 2026: Funding Opportunity for Environmental and Climate Projects in Mozambique appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Michigan Environmental Justice Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026: Fully Detailed Opportunity for Humanities and Environmental Justice Scholars
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The University of Michigan School for Environment and Sustainability (SEAS) has announced an outstanding opportunity for emerging scholars passionate about humanities research, climate justice, environmental justice, and public impact. Applications are now open for the Environmental Justice Postdoctoral Fellow position under the newly established Environmental Justice + Humanities Hub. This prestigious fellowship is designed for […]

The post University of Michigan Environmental Justice Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026: Fully Detailed Opportunity for Humanities and Environmental Justice Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Michigan School for Environment and Sustainability (SEAS) has announced an outstanding opportunity for emerging scholars passionate about humanities research, climate justice, environmental justice, and public impact. Applications are now open for the Environmental Justice Postdoctoral Fellow position under the newly established Environmental Justice + Humanities Hub.

This prestigious fellowship is designed for scholars who want to advance academic leadership at the intersection of environmental justice and the humanities while contributing to groundbreaking research, mentorship, curriculum development, and community-centered environmental impact initiatives.

The fellowship is supported by the Andrew W. Mellon Foundation and offers postdoctoral researchers the chance to work alongside renowned environmental justice scholars and interdisciplinary leaders at one of the world’s leading public research universities.

About the Environmental Justice + Humanities Hub

The Environmental Justice + Humanities Hub was officially launched in December 2024 as a transformative initiative dedicated to integrating humanities scholarship into environmental justice research, education, and public engagement.

The Hub seeks to strengthen the role of humanities perspectives in addressing critical environmental challenges such as:

  • Climate change
  • Environmental racism
  • Pollution
  • Biodiversity loss
  • Land dispossession
  • Environment-based insecurities
  • Indigenous environmental issues
  • Community resilience and sustainability

The initiative emphasizes both local and global environmental justice engagement. While rooted in Michigan and the broader Midwest region of the United States, the Hub also fosters international collaboration and global scholarly connections.

The Hub aims to create lasting social impact through:

  1. Humanistic research
  2. Public scholarship
  3. Mentorship programs
  4. Community partnerships
  5. Environmental justice education
  6. Curriculum innovation
  7. Interdisciplinary collaboration

Fellowship Overview

The Environmental Justice Postdoctoral Fellowship offers scholars the opportunity to deepen their research while becoming active contributors to a vibrant intellectual community focused on environmental justice and humanities scholarship.

The selected fellow will join a rapidly growing network of students, faculty, staff, and researchers who are advancing environmental justice education and scholarship at the University of Michigan.

This position is especially attractive for early-career academics because it provides:

  • Dedicated research time
  • Strong institutional mentorship
  • Leadership opportunities
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Curriculum development experience
  • Community engagement opportunities
  • Academic career acceleration support

Unlike many postdoctoral positions, this fellowship does not include teaching obligations, allowing fellows to focus more extensively on research and professional development.

Research and Academic Focus Areas

Applicants from a wide range of humanities disciplines are encouraged to apply, provided their work strongly connects to environmental justice.

Potential academic backgrounds may include:

  • Environmental humanities
  • Indigenous studies
  • History
  • Literature
  • Philosophy
  • Anthropology
  • Cultural studies
  • Religious studies
  • Political theory
  • Ethnic studies
  • Gender studies
  • Public humanities
  • Media studies
  • Geography
  • Arts-based environmental research

The University of Michigan is particularly interested in candidates whose research demonstrates:

  • Strong humanities methodologies
  • Commitment to environmental justice
  • Public-facing scholarship
  • Collaborative engagement
  • Educational innovation
  • Interdisciplinary thinking

Mentorship and Leadership Structure

The selected fellow will work directly under the mentorship of renowned environmental justice scholar Dr. Kyle Whyte.

The fellow will also receive advisory support from:

  • Sara Soderstrom
  • Sarah Nahar
  • Eric Hemenway

This mentorship structure provides fellows with access to interdisciplinary guidance, academic networking opportunities, and collaborative project development.

What the Selected Fellow Will Do

The Environmental Justice Postdoctoral Fellow will play an active leadership role within the Hub and broader University of Michigan environmental justice community.

Key responsibilities include:

1. Conduct Independent Research

The fellow will continue advancing their own scholarly research agenda throughout the fellowship period.

This includes:

  • Publishing academic work
  • Developing future research projects
  • Building academic collaborations
  • Strengthening professional visibility

2. Develop New Environmental Justice Projects

The fellow will collaborate with faculty, staff, and students to design and oversee at least one major project that contributes to the growth and impact of the EJ + Humanities Hub.

Projects may include:

  • Community engagement initiatives
  • Research collaborations
  • Public humanities programs
  • Environmental storytelling projects
  • Curriculum innovation
  • Policy engagement activities

3. Mentor Students

The fellow will support undergraduate and graduate students working on environmental justice and humanities-related projects.

Mentorship responsibilities may involve:

  • Research advising
  • Project guidance
  • Professional development support
  • Collaborative learning opportunities

4. Participate in Leadership and Planning

The selected candidate will serve on advisory and planning committees associated with the Environmental Justice + Humanities Hub.

This provides meaningful institutional leadership experience for early-career scholars.

Why This Fellowship Stands Out

This opportunity is especially valuable because it combines:

  • Research freedom
  • Institutional support
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Environmental justice leadership
  • Humanities scholarship
  • Public impact
  • Mentorship opportunities

Additionally, the absence of teaching requirements allows fellows to concentrate on building a stronger publication record and developing innovative research projects.

The University of Michigan also emphasizes intentional mentoring throughout the fellowship to help accelerate the fellow’s long-term academic career trajectory.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for this fellowship, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Hold a Ph.D. in any academic field
  • Complete the Ph.D. within four months of the fellowship start date
  • Demonstrate strong humanities-focused research
  • Show commitment to environmental justice scholarship
  • Demonstrate interest in humanities education
  • Possess leadership potential within environmental justice communities

Although the Ph.D. field is open, the candidate’s dissertation and primary research areas must substantially focus on humanities scholarship.

Required Application Materials

Applicants must submit all materials in a single PDF document.

Required documents include:

  1. Cover letter describing:
    • Relevant experience
    • Scholarly interests
    • Specific interest in the fellowship
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Writing sample or artistic production
    • Published or unpublished
    • Article-length preferred
  4. Names and contact information of three references

The cover letter is especially important and should clearly explain how the applicant’s background aligns with the fellowship’s goals.

Application Review Process

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis throughout the posting period.

The University of Michigan notes that:

  • The position may be filled at any time after the minimum posting period
  • Early applications are strongly encouraged
  • Review continues until the position is filled

Application Deadline

No fixed final deadline has been announced.

However:

  • Applications are reviewed as they are received
  • The position can close once filled
  • Interested candidates should apply as early as possible

Click Here for more and to apply

Work Arrangement

The fellowship may include hybrid or remote work flexibility depending on departmental approval and annual review processes.

Work mode arrangements are subject to institutional policies and may change over time.

Equal Opportunity Commitment

The University of Michigan is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering diversity, equity, inclusion, and justice within higher education and research.

Final Thoughts

For scholars passionate about environmental justice, humanities research, climate justice, Indigenous studies, and public scholarship, this postdoctoral fellowship represents a rare opportunity to grow within a world-class research institution while contributing to meaningful social and environmental transformation.

The Environmental Justice + Humanities Hub at the University of Michigan is building a powerful interdisciplinary future, and this fellowship places emerging scholars at the center of that mission.

For more global fellowship opportunities click Here

The post University of Michigan Environmental Justice Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026: Fully Detailed Opportunity for Humanities and Environmental Justice Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WINGS Project Management Consultancy 2026: Global Part-Time Consultancy Opportunity with Up to USD 300 Daily Rate
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The global philanthropy ecosystem continues to expand as organizations and institutions seek innovative ways to create sustainable social impact, strengthen international cooperation, and support global development goals. In response to these evolving needs, WINGS (Worldwide Initiatives for Grantmaker Support) has officially opened applications for its highly anticipated Project Management Consultancy 2026. This international consultancy opportunity […]

The post WINGS Project Management Consultancy 2026: Global Part-Time Consultancy Opportunity with Up to USD 300 Daily Rate appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The global philanthropy ecosystem continues to expand as organizations and institutions seek innovative ways to create sustainable social impact, strengthen international cooperation, and support global development goals. In response to these evolving needs, WINGS (Worldwide Initiatives for Grantmaker Support) has officially opened applications for its highly anticipated Project Management Consultancy 2026.

This international consultancy opportunity is designed for experienced project management and policy professionals who are passionate about advocacy, philanthropy, international cooperation, and multi-stakeholder partnerships. The selected consultant will support strategic EU FFPA policy and advocacy initiatives while collaborating with one of the world’s leading philanthropy support networks.

The consultancy offers a flexible part-time engagement, international exposure, and a competitive daily rate of up to USD 300 per day.

Applications close on 15 May 2026 at 12 PM UTC.

About WINGS

WINGS (Worldwide Initiatives for Grantmaker Support) is the only global network dedicated to strengthening philanthropy support and development organizations worldwide. The organization works to ensure that philanthropy remains well-resourced, collaborative, and capable of driving systemic change across communities and countries.

WINGS currently connects:

  • More than 250 member organizations
  • Institutions across 60+ countries
  • Global thought leaders and changemakers
  • Philanthropy support organizations worldwide

The organization is committed to:

  • Building a more just and equitable world
  • Supporting sustainable development
  • Strengthening philanthropy ecosystems globally
  • Encouraging collective action and collaboration
  • Advancing the 2030 Agenda and Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

Through policy advocacy, knowledge-sharing, and strategic partnerships, WINGS continues to play a critical role in helping philanthropy contribute meaningfully to global transformation.

Background of the Consultancy Programme

According to the official Terms of Reference, WINGS is currently implementing a multi-year EU FFPA programme focused on several major objectives, including:

  1. Strengthening the enabling environment for philanthropy
  2. Amplifying philanthropy’s collective voice in multilateral policy spaces
  3. Supporting philanthropy engagement in global platforms such as the G20
  4. Building the capacity of governments and philanthropic actors through multi-stakeholder partnerships (MSPs)

As the programme advances into a critical implementation stage, WINGS seeks a Senior Project Management Consultant who can strengthen coordination across consultant-led workstreams and ensure timely delivery of strategic outputs.

The consultant will work closely with:

  • The Director of Collective Intelligence and Advocacy
  • The Head of Policy and Advocacy

The consultancy is intended to establish sustainable coordination systems that can continue functioning internally even after the consultancy period ends.

Duration and Scope of the Consultancy

The consultancy is structured as:

  • An 8–10 month engagement
  • Part-time consultancy work
  • Estimated commitment of up to 8 days per month

Monthly priorities and engagement levels will be coordinated directly with the WINGS leadership team to ensure flexibility and effective implementation.

The consultant will also participate in regular coordination and alignment meetings to monitor progress and delivery.

Main Responsibilities of the Consultant

The selected consultant will play a strategic role in supporting several policy and advocacy workstreams.

1. Coordination Across MSP Workstreams

The consultant will support coordination of:

  • MSP research initiatives
  • E-learning workstreams
  • MSP pilots
  • Enabling Environment Guide activities

Responsibilities include:

  • Aligning timelines and deliverables
  • Monitoring dependencies across projects
  • Ensuring consistency in outputs and messaging
  • Identifying risks, overlaps, or gaps
  • Recommending practical adjustments

2. Design and Delivery Tracking

A significant aspect of the consultancy involves creating practical project management systems.

Responsibilities include:

  • Developing workplans and tracking systems
  • Supporting management of e-learning and MSP research providers
  • Creating reporting frameworks and templates
  • Monitoring milestones and deliverables
  • Conducting strategic quality reviews of outputs

The consultant will focus on improving coherence, usability, and implementation efficiency rather than line-by-line editorial work.

3. EU Engagement Support

The consultant will provide support for WINGS’ engagement with EU processes and stakeholders by:

  • Monitoring EU developments
  • Supporting preparation of briefings and updates
  • Aligning deliverables with EU expectations and timelines
  • Coordinating workshops and consultations

This component of the role is especially important for professionals with experience in EU-funded programmes and international policy engagement.

4. Internal Coordination and Reporting

The consultant will serve as a key coordination point between internal teams and external providers.

Responsibilities include preparing:

  • Progress updates
  • Risk assessments
  • Leadership decision briefs
  • Internal planning and reporting documentation

The role also involves supporting reporting linked to EU FFPA programme requirements.

Expected Deliverables

The selected consultant will contribute to several important outputs, including:

  • Consolidated workplans and milestone trackers
  • Internal coordination and delivery-tracking tools
  • Progress and risk assessment reports
  • Inputs into EU engagement processes
  • Handover documentation for internal teams

These deliverables are intended to strengthen operational sustainability and improve internal management systems within WINGS.

Required Skills and Expertise

WINGS is looking for highly organized professionals with strong experience in international programme management and policy coordination.

Applicants should possess:

  • Up to 7 years of relevant project or programme management experience
  • Experience managing consultant-heavy portfolios
  • Strong communication and stakeholder-management abilities
  • Experience designing practical coordination systems
  • Familiarity with EU-funded programmes and reporting structures
  • Ability to manage multiple parallel workstreams effectively

Experience within:

  • Philanthropy
  • Civil society organizations
  • International development
  • Policy advocacy sectors

is considered an advantage.

Consultancy Fee and Payment Structure

The consultancy offers a highly competitive compensation package.

Payment Details:

  • Daily consultancy fee: Up to USD 300 per day
  • Payments made monthly
  • Estimated workload: Up to 8 days per month

This structure provides flexibility while ensuring meaningful professional engagement with a globally respected organization.

Application Process

Applicants are required to submit their proposals through the official online application form.

Important Application Information:

  • Deadline: Friday, 15 May 2026 at 12 PM UTC
  • Applications submitted outside the online form will not be accepted
  • Interviews may take place during the last week of May 2026
  • Selected consultants are expected to begin work in early June 2026

Eligibility Criteria

Due to EU funding requirements under the EU NDICI-Global Europe Regulation, the consultancy follows specific eligibility rules.

Important Eligibility Information:

  • Legal entities must be established in eligible countries under EU Regulation (EU 2021/947)
  • Independent experts and freelance consultants are eligible regardless of nationality

This makes the opportunity accessible to a broad range of qualified international professionals.

Why You Should Apply

This consultancy presents a unique opportunity for professionals seeking:

  • International consultancy experience
  • Exposure to global philanthropy ecosystems
  • EU policy engagement experience
  • Flexible part-time consultancy work
  • Opportunities to collaborate with global stakeholders
  • Strategic leadership exposure

Working with WINGS also provides valuable networking opportunities with international organizations, advocacy professionals, and philanthropy leaders worldwide.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can access the official opportunity page and application form below:

 

Apply here

 

Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong proposals highlighting their experience in project coordination, policy engagement, stakeholder management, and international programme implementation.

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post WINGS Project Management Consultancy 2026: Global Part-Time Consultancy Opportunity with Up to USD 300 Daily Rate appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AREF Essential Grant-Writing Skills Programme 2026 for African Health Researchers: Fully Online Training for Early-Career Scientists
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Africa Research Excellence Fund (AREF) has officially opened applications for the Essential Grant-Writing Skills Programme 2026, a highly competitive and intensive online training programme designed for emerging African researchers working in health and biomedical sciences. This unique programme is specifically tailored to help early-career African scientists develop the skills required to write competitive research […]

The post AREF Essential Grant-Writing Skills Programme 2026 for African Health Researchers: Fully Online Training for Early-Career Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Africa Research Excellence Fund (AREF) has officially opened applications for the Essential Grant-Writing Skills Programme 2026, a highly competitive and intensive online training programme designed for emerging African researchers working in health and biomedical sciences.

This unique programme is specifically tailored to help early-career African scientists develop the skills required to write competitive research proposals and fellowship applications capable of attracting international, regional, and national funding.

For researchers who are striving to transition toward research independence and secure major grants for impactful health research in Africa, this programme offers an invaluable opportunity to strengthen proposal development skills, understand funding expectations, and improve long-term research success.

About the AREF Essential Grant-Writing Skills Programme

The Essential Grant-Writing Skills Programme is a fully online professional development initiative created by the Africa Research Excellence Fund to strengthen Africa’s health research ecosystem.

The programme is designed for researchers who:

  • Are in the early stages of their research careers
  • Are actively involved in health or biomedical research
  • Are seeking independent research funding
  • Have secured smaller grants previously but need support to win larger competitive grants

The training focuses on equipping participants with practical grant-writing expertise and proposal development strategies that can significantly improve funding success rates.

Why This Programme Matters

One of the major barriers faced by researchers across Africa is limited access to structured grant-writing mentorship and proposal development training.

Many talented researchers possess excellent scientific ideas but struggle to:

  • Develop compelling proposals
  • Align applications with funder priorities
  • Present research ideas effectively
  • Prepare competitive budgets and project plans
  • Navigate peer review expectations

AREF’s programme directly addresses these gaps by offering structured, practical, and interactive learning experiences.

Programme Aim

The programme aims to help talented African health researchers:

  1. Build high-level grant-writing skills
  2. Develop competitive fellowship and research proposals
  3. Improve understanding of international funding systems
  4. Increase success in securing research grants
  5. Strengthen long-term scientific leadership capacity in Africa

What Participants Will Learn

The programme covers a broad range of proposal development and research funding topics.

Core Training Areas

1. Developing Your Research Niche

Participants will learn how to:

  • Define a unique research identity
  • Position their work strategically
  • Build a strong scientific profile

2. Formulating a Strong Research Question

The training explains how to:

  • Identify impactful research problems
  • Create compelling research objectives
  • Develop fundable research questions

3. Understanding Funding Requirements

Researchers will gain insights into:

  • What funders look for
  • How grants are evaluated
  • Common mistakes in applications

4. Writing in Plain English

The programme emphasizes:

  • Clear scientific communication
  • Simplifying complex ideas
  • Writing persuasive applications

5. Writing Proposal Sections

Participants will receive guidance on:

  • Project summaries
  • Methodology sections
  • Case for support
  • Project descriptions

6. Budget and Project Planning

Researchers will learn:

  • Budget preparation techniques
  • Timeline development
  • Project management strategies

7. Collaboration and Team Management

The programme also explores:

  • Building research partnerships
  • Managing collaborations effectively
  • Team leadership principles

8. Peer Review Practice

Participants will:

  • Learn how peer review works
  • Review colleagues’ proposals
  • Improve critical evaluation skills

9. Fellowship Interview Preparation

The training includes preparation for:

  • Research fellowship interviews
  • Communicating research impact
  • Presenting confidently to funders

Programme Structure and Schedule

The programme is divided into two intensive online phases.

Phase One

7 – 11 September 2026

  • Daily online sessions
  • Time: 08:30 – 11:30 GMT

Phase Two

16 – 18 November 2026

  • Daily online sessions
  • Time: 08:30 – 11:30 GMT

Between the two phases, participants will complete:

  • A mini grant proposal
  • Peer-review exercises
  • Independent proposal-writing assignments

Learning Format

The programme will be delivered entirely online through Zoom and includes:

  • Interactive lectures
  • Group discussions
  • Online teamwork
  • Offline subgroup assignments
  • Preparatory exercises
  • Proposal-writing activities

All sessions will be conducted in English.

What the Programme Does Not Cover
