AREF clearly states that the programme will not focus on:

  • Research ethics approval procedures
  • Intellectual property management
  • Completing specific funder application forms
  • General research communication training

Additionally, facilitators will not directly write or edit participants’ personal grant proposals.

Participant Expectations

AREF expects selected participants to fully commit to the programme.

Mandatory Requirements

Participants must:

  • Attend all online sessions
  • Participate actively in discussions and exercises
  • Complete preparatory reading and assignments
  • Dedicate additional time to proposal writing
  • Engage in peer-review activities

Failure to attend all sessions may result in participants not receiving a certificate of completion.

Technical Requirements

Applicants should ideally have:

  • Reliable internet access
  • A laptop or computer compatible with Zoom
  • Ability to use a smartphone alongside Zoom sessions

AREF does not provide funding for internet access, equipment, or participant-related expenses.

Eligible Research Fields

AREF welcomes applications from researchers working in:

  • Biomedicine
  • Physiology and pharmacology
  • Clinical sciences
  • Epidemiology
  • Mathematics and statistics
  • Environmental health
  • Veterinary science (“One Health”)
  • Public health
  • Mental health
  • Behavioural sciences
  • Data science for health
  • Social and economic sciences
  • Health policy and systems research

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must provide evidence that they:

  1. Are citizens of an African country
  2. Are early-career researchers
  3. Hold either:
    • A PhD/DPhil/MD awarded on or after 1 January 2020
    • OR a medical/clinical qualification plus a research-related Master’s degree awarded on or after 1 January 2020
  4. Are employed in Africa by:
    • A recognized university
    • A specialist research institution
  5. Are actively involved in health research
  6. Have not previously attended equivalent grant-writing training
  7. Do not already hold international grants totaling £250,000 or more

Selection Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Research achievements and outputs
  • Potential to benefit from the training
  • Long-term scientific vision
  • Commitment to addressing African health challenges
  • Ability to share acquired knowledge with others
  • English language proficiency

AREF also promotes:

  • Gender balance
  • Language inclusivity
  • Institutional diversity

No more than two participants will be selected from the same institution.

Application Deadline

22 May 2026

AREF aims to notify successful applicants by 8 July 2026.

How to Apply

Interested applicants must apply through the official AREF portal.

Applicants are required to:

  1. Register an account on the portal
  2. Access and complete the application form
  3. Review the application guidance and FAQs before submission

Apply HERE

Key Dates

Activity                                        Date

Call Launch                            27 April 2026

Application Deadline            22 May 2026

Notification of Outcome        8 July 2026

Final Thoughts

The AREF Essential Grant-Writing Skills Programme 2026 is more than just a training opportunity—it is a strategic investment in the future of African health research leadership.

For emerging scientists seeking to build competitive funding skills, strengthen proposal development expertise, and accelerate their research careers, this programme provides the mentorship, structure, and professional development necessary for long-term success.

Researchers committed to solving Africa’s pressing health challenges should strongly consider applying before the deadline.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT

The post AREF Essential Grant-Writing Skills Programme 2026 for African Health Researchers: Fully Online Training for Early-Career Scientists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD in Parenting and Hope at the University of Amsterdam 2026
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for a fully funded PhD position in Parenting in Times of Uncertainty at the University of Amsterdam, Faculty of Social and Behavioural Sciences. This exciting doctoral opportunity is part of the ERC-funded PARENTALHOPE project, which explores how hope shapes parenting behaviour and decision-making in uncertain environments. The research seeks to understand […]

The post Fully Funded PhD in Parenting and Hope at the University of Amsterdam 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for a fully funded PhD position in Parenting in Times of Uncertainty at the University of Amsterdam, Faculty of Social and Behavioural Sciences.

This exciting doctoral opportunity is part of the ERC-funded PARENTALHOPE project, which explores how hope shapes parenting behaviour and decision-making in uncertain environments. The research seeks to understand why some parents become more protective during uncertain times, while others encourage exploration and independence in their children.

The selected PhD candidate will join a leading international research environment at the Research Institute of Child Development and Education (RICDE) and work closely with renowned researcher Prof. Patty Leijten.

About the PhD Project

The project focuses on the psychology of hope, parenting, and uncertainty across different cultural contexts. It aims to investigate how parents respond when children’s futures feel unpredictable and what psychological mechanisms influence these parenting choices.

This research will contribute to broader discussions in developmental psychology, family studies, and behavioural science.

What the PhD Candidate Will Do

The successful applicant will:

  • Design and manage a cross-national longitudinal study across the Netherlands, Estonia, and Portugal
  • Conduct experimental laboratory studies
  • Analyse advanced quantitative datasets
  • Publish academic papers in peer-reviewed journals
  • Present findings at international conferences and events

Research Focus Areas

The PhD will examine questions such as:

  • When do parents become more protective?
  • When do parents encourage exploration and independence?
  • How does hope influence parenting behaviour under uncertainty?

Funding and Benefits

The University of Amsterdam offers a highly competitive package, including:

  • Fully funded 4-year PhD contract
  • Initial 1-year contract with extension to 3 additional years upon satisfactory performance
  • Monthly salary ranging from €3,059 to €3,881
  • Holiday allowance and additional bonuses
  • Graduate training and professional development opportunities
  • International research exposure and collaboration

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants should have:

  • A master’s degree in:
    • Psychology (developmental, social, family psychology)
    • Pedagogy
    • Or a related field
  • Strong quantitative research and analytical skills
  • Interest in parenting behaviour and theory-driven research
  • Scientific writing experience
  • Ability to work collaboratively in research teams

Why Study at the University of Amsterdam?

The University of Amsterdam is internationally recognized for excellence in psychology, behavioural sciences, and education research. Students benefit from a highly collaborative and innovative academic environment with strong international partnerships.

Application Deadline

Applications close on:

17 May 2026

Start Date

The PhD position is expected to begin in:

September 2026

How to Apply

Applicants are required to submit:

  • CV
  • Motivation letter
  • Writing sample (thesis or research paper)

Apply here

For More Scholarship Opportunities , Visit OFY Here.

The post Fully Funded PhD in Parenting and Hope at the University of Amsterdam 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
